name: Baton Circuit Router
description: Async circuit routing engine. Takes classified DiffHunks and routes them through configured review stages per CircuitConfig. Supports parallel execution of independent stages via asyncio.gather, sequential execution where ordering matters. Implements circuit-breaker pattern: a failing/timing-out reviewer produces a degraded Assessment (recorded as partial result) rather than crashing the pipeline. Emits Chronicler events at stage boundaries (stage.started, stage.complete). Returns ordered list of Assessments from all stages. Configurable timeout per stage. Deterministic output ordering matches CircuitConfig.stages list regardless of parallel execution timing.

types.name: ReviewStage
types.description: Named stage in the review circuit pipeline.

types.name: StageTimeoutMs

fields.name: value
fields.description: Timeout duration in milliseconds.

validators.error_message: Stage timeout must be between 100ms and 300000ms (5 minutes).

types.description: Timeout in milliseconds for a single reviewer stage invocation. Must be a positive integer between 100ms and 300000ms (5 minutes).

types.name: ParallelGroup

fields.name: stages
fields.description: Stages in this parallel group. Must contain at least 2 stages (a single stage needs no parallelism). All must be members of CircuitConfig.stages.

validators.error_message: A parallel group must contain at least 2 stages.

types.description: A group of ReviewStage values that may execute concurrently via asyncio.gather. All stages in a group must be present in CircuitConfig.stages. Frozen Pydantic model.

types.name: CircuitConfig

fields.name: stages
fields.description: Ordered list of review stages to execute.

fields.name: parallel_stages
fields.description: Groups of stages that can run in parallel. Each inner list runs concurrently.

fields.name: stage_timeout_ms
fields.description: Default timeout per stage in milliseconds.

fields.name: block_threshold
fields.description: Minimum number of critical/high findings to trigger a block decision.

fields.name: warn_threshold
fields.description: Minimum number of medium findings to trigger a warn decision.

types.description: Configuration for Baton circuit routing: stage ordering, parallelism, and timeouts.

types.name: ExecutionStep

fields.name: stages
fields.description: One or more stages to execute in this step. If len > 1, stages execute concurrently via asyncio.gather.

validators.error_message: An execution step must contain at least one stage.

fields.name: is_parallel
fields.description: True if this step executes its stages concurrently.

types.description: A single step in the resolved execution plan. Either a single sequential stage or a parallel group. Internal type built at construction time.

types.name: ExecutionPlan

fields.name: steps
fields.description: Ordered execution steps. Steps execute sequentially; stages within a parallel step execute concurrently.

validators.error_message: Execution plan must have at least one step.

fields.name: stage_order
fields.description: Canonical ordering of all stages, matching CircuitConfig.stages. Used to sort final Assessment list deterministically.

types.description: The resolved execution plan: an ordered list of ExecutionSteps derived from CircuitConfig at construction time. Frozen Pydantic model.

types.name: ChroniclerEventType
types.description: Well-known Chronicler event types emitted throughout the review lifecycle.

types.name: ChroniclerEvent

fields.name: event_id
fields.description: Unique event identifier.

fields.name: event_type
fields.description: Well-known event type.

fields.name: review_request_id
fields.description: ID of the ReviewRequest this event relates to.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the event occurred.

fields.name: stage
fields.description: Review stage this event relates to, if applicable.

fields.name: reviewer_id
fields.description: Reviewer agent ID if this event is reviewer-specific.

fields.name: payload
fields.description: Arbitrary structured data associated with the event.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of the event.

types.description: A structured event emitted at review lifecycle boundaries for audit trail and story assembly.

types.name: ChroniclerEmitter

fields.name: emit
fields.description: Async method to emit a ChroniclerEvent. Implementations should be fire-and-forget safe.

types.description: Protocol (runtime_checkable) for Chronicler event emission. Implementations must provide an async emit method. Defined in circuit.py for decoupling from chronicle module.

types.name: ClockProvider

fields.name: now_utc
fields.description: Returns current UTC timestamp as ISO 8601 string ending in Z.

fields.name: monotonic_ms
fields.description: Returns monotonic clock value in milliseconds for duration measurement.

types.description: Protocol (runtime_checkable) for injectable time and ID generation. Allows deterministic testing by replacing real clocks with fixed values.

types.name: CircuitConfigError

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of the configuration error.

fields.name: missing_reviewers
fields.description: Stages in config that have no registered reviewer. Empty if the error is not about missing reviewers.

fields.name: invalid_parallel_stages
fields.description: Parallel group stages not found in config.stages. Empty if the error is not about parallel subset violation.

types.description: Exception raised at BatonCircuitRouter construction time when CircuitConfig validation fails. Subclass of Exception.

types.name: ReviewRequest

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique review request identifier.

fields.name: source

fields.name: hunks

fields.name: file_paths

fields.name: created_at
fields.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of when the request was created.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Arbitrary key-value metadata (PR number, branch, author, etc.).

types.name: DiffHunk

fields.name: id

fields.name: file_path

fields.name: start_line_old

fields.name: count_old

fields.name: start_line_new
fields.description: Starting line number in the new file.

fields.name: count_new
fields.description: Number of lines in the new file span.

fields.name: context_before

fields.name: added_lines

fields.name: removed_lines

fields.name: context_after

fields.name: raw_header

fields.name: classifications

fields.name: language

types.name: Severity

types.name: FindingCategory

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique finding identifier.

fields.name: hunk_id

fields.name: file_path
fields.description: File path where the finding was detected.

fields.name: line_number
fields.description: Specific line number if applicable.

fields.name: severity
fields.description: Severity of the finding.

fields.name: confidence
fields.description: Reviewer confidence in this finding.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Short human-readable title of the finding.

fields.name: description

fields.name: suggestion
fields.description: Suggested fix or remediation.

fields.name: rule_id
fields.description: Identifier of the rule or pattern that triggered this finding.

fields.name: stage

types.description: A single issue found by a reviewer in a specific hunk.

types.name: Assessment

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique assessment identifier.

fields.name: review_request_id

fields.name: stage

fields.name: reviewer_id

fields.name: decision

fields.name: findings

fields.name: confidence

fields.name: is_partial

fields.name: error_message

fields.name: duration_ms

fields.name: created_at

types.description: A single reviewer's complete assessment of the diff hunks it was given.

types.name: PACTKey

fields.name: value
fields.description: Dotted PACT key for Sentinel attribution.

validators.error_message: PACT key must follow exemplar.module.Class.method pattern.

types.description: A PACT attribution key string. Module-level constants following the pattern 'exemplar.module.Class.method'.

types.name: Confidence
types.description: Confidence level of a reviewer finding or merged assessment.

types.name: ReviewDecision

types.name: ClassificationLabel
types.description: Ledger data classification label for diff content.

types.name: ReviewerProtocol
types.description: Auto-stubbed type — referenced but not defined in contract 'circuit'

functions.name: __init__
functions.description: Construct a BatonCircuitRouter. Validates that every stage in config.stages has a corresponding entry in the reviewers dict, that all parallel_stages entries are subsets of config.stages, and that no stage appears in more than one parallel group. Resolves the execution plan from config topology at construction time. Raises CircuitConfigError on validation failure.

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: Circuit routing configuration defining stages, parallel groups, and timeouts.

inputs.name: reviewers
inputs.description: Mapping from each configured review stage to its reviewer implementation. Must cover every stage in config.stages.

inputs.name: chronicler
inputs.description: Optional Chronicler emitter for stage boundary events. If None, no events are emitted.

inputs.name: clock
inputs.description: Optional clock provider for injectable timestamps and duration measurement. If None, uses real system clock.

error_cases.name: missing_reviewers
error_cases.condition: One or more stages in config.stages have no corresponding key in the reviewers dict

error_data.missing_reviewers: list of ReviewStage values without registered reviewers

error_cases.name: invalid_parallel_subset
error_cases.condition: A ParallelGroup in config.parallel_stages contains stages not present in config.stages

error_data.invalid_parallel_stages: list of ReviewStage values not in config.stages

error_cases.name: duplicate_parallel_membership
error_cases.condition: A ReviewStage appears in more than one ParallelGroup

error_data.message: reviewers dict must not be empty

preconditions: config is a valid CircuitConfig instance (passes Pydantic validation)
All ReviewerProtocol values satisfy the runtime_checkable protocol
postconditions: self._execution_plan is a resolved ExecutionPlan covering all stages in config.stages exactly once
self._config, self._reviewers, self._chronicler, self._clock are stored immutably
No I/O has been performed (construction is synchronous and pure aside from validation)

structured_side_effects.description: Construction is synchronous and performs no I/O. Only validates config and builds execution plan in memory.

functions.name: run
functions.description: Execute the full review circuit for a given ReviewRequest. Iterates through the resolved ExecutionPlan: for each step, invokes reviewer(s) — concurrently for parallel steps, sequentially otherwise. Each reviewer invocation is wrapped in asyncio.wait_for with the configured stage timeout. On timeout or exception, a degraded Assessment (is_partial=True) is produced instead of propagating the error. Emits Chronicler events (stage.started, stage.complete) at each stage boundary via fire-and-forget. Returns assessments sorted in the canonical order defined by CircuitConfig.stages. PACT key: exemplar.circuit.BatonCircuitRouter.run

inputs.name: request
inputs.description: The review request containing classified diff hunks to route through all configured stages.

error_cases.name: invalid_request
error_cases.condition: request is None or fails ReviewRequest validation

error_data.message: ReviewRequest must be non-None and valid
ReviewRequest must contain at least one DiffHunk

preconditions: request is a valid ReviewRequest with at least one DiffHunk
postconditions: Returned list has exactly len(config.stages) Assessments, one per configured stage
Assessments are ordered by their position in CircuitConfig.stages
Every Assessment has a valid ReviewStage matching a configured stage
No exceptions are propagated from reviewer invocations — all failures produce is_partial=True assessments
If Chronicler is configured, stage.started and stage.complete events were emitted (fire-and-forget) for each stage
Each Assessment.duration_ms reflects the actual wall-clock time of that reviewer invocation
side_effects: Invokes ReviewerProtocol.review() for each configured stage
Emits ChroniclerEvent via ChroniclerEmitter.emit() at stage boundaries (fire-and-forget)
Uses asyncio.wait_for for timeout enforcement per stage

structured_side_effects.description: Invokes each registered reviewer's async review method. Wrapped in circuit-breaker timeout. Reviewer implementations may perform network I/O internally.
Emits stage.started and stage.complete events to Chronicler. Fire-and-forget: exceptions from emit are caught and logged, never propagated.
Logs warnings on reviewer timeouts/failures and on Chronicler emission failures.

performance_budget.complexity: O(S) where S = number of configured stages; parallel groups reduce wall-clock time but not total work

functions.name: resolve_execution_plan
functions.description: Resolve the CircuitConfig into an ordered ExecutionPlan. Groups stages from parallel_stages into concurrent ExecutionSteps and remaining stages into sequential steps, preserving the order defined in config.stages. Called once at construction time. PACT key: exemplar.circuit.BatonCircuitRouter.resolve_execution_plan

inputs.name: config
inputs.description: The validated circuit configuration to resolve into an execution plan.

preconditions: config has passed all CircuitConfig validation (no duplicates, valid parallel groups)
This is called after reviewer registry validation succeeds
postconditions: Every stage in config.stages appears in exactly one ExecutionStep
ExecutionPlan.stage_order matches config.stages exactly
Parallel groups are preserved as multi-stage ExecutionSteps with is_parallel=True
Sequential stages appear as single-stage ExecutionSteps with is_parallel=False
Step ordering respects the position of the earliest stage in each group within config.stages

structured_side_effects.description: Pure function that computes an execution plan from config. No I/O or side effects.

functions.name: invoke_reviewer
functions.description: Invoke a single reviewer with circuit-breaker protection. Wraps the reviewer's async review call in asyncio.wait_for with the configured stage timeout. On TimeoutError or any Exception, returns a degraded Assessment with is_partial=True and the error message. Emits stage.started before invocation and stage.complete after (success or degraded). PACT key: exemplar.circuit.BatonCircuitRouter.invoke_reviewer

inputs.name: request
inputs.description: The review request to pass to the reviewer.

inputs.name: timeout_ms
inputs.description: Timeout for this reviewer invocation in milliseconds.

preconditions: stage has a registered reviewer in self._reviewers
postconditions: Returns exactly one Assessment for the given stage
Assessment.stage == stage
If reviewer completed successfully within timeout: Assessment.is_partial == False and Assessment.findings contains reviewer output
If reviewer timed out or threw: Assessment.is_partial == True and Assessment.error_message describes the failure
Assessment.duration_ms reflects actual invocation wall-clock time
Chronicler events emitted (fire-and-forget) regardless of success/failure
No exception is ever propagated from this method
side_effects: Emits ChroniclerEvent stage.started and stage.complete via fire-and-forget

structured_side_effects.description: Invokes the reviewer's async review method, wrapped in asyncio.wait_for for timeout enforcement.
Fire-and-forget emission of stage.started and stage.complete events.
Logs warning on reviewer timeout/failure and on Chronicler emission failure.

performance_budget.complexity: O(1) per invocation — delegates to a single reviewer

functions.name: get_stage_timeout
functions.description: Resolve the effective timeout for a given stage. Returns the stage-specific override from config.stage_timeouts if present, otherwise config.default_timeout_ms. PACT key: exemplar.circuit.BatonCircuitRouter.get_stage_timeout

inputs.name: stage
inputs.description: The review stage to look up timeout for.

preconditions: stage is a member of self._config.stages
postconditions: Returns config.stage_timeouts[stage] if stage is in stage_timeouts, else config.default_timeout_ms
Returned value satisfies StageTimeoutMs validation (100-300000)

structured_side_effects.description: Pure lookup function with no side effects.

functions.name: get_execution_plan
functions.description: Returns the resolved ExecutionPlan for inspection and testing. Read-only accessor. PACT key: exemplar.circuit.BatonCircuitRouter.get_execution_plan
postconditions: Returns the immutable ExecutionPlan resolved at construction time
Returned plan is a frozen Pydantic model — caller cannot mutate it

structured_side_effects.description: Pure read-only accessor with no side effects.

invariants: BatonCircuitRouter never propagates exceptions from reviewer invocations — all failures are absorbed into degraded Assessments with is_partial=True
BatonCircuitRouter never propagates exceptions from ChroniclerEmitter.emit — all emission failures are caught and logged (fire-and-forget)
The returned list of Assessments from run() always has exactly len(config.stages) elements, one per configured stage, in config.stages order
No reviewer invocation exceeds its configured timeout — asyncio.wait_for enforces cancellation
The ExecutionPlan is immutable after construction and determines all routing behavior
Every stage in config.stages has a registered reviewer (enforced at construction time via CircuitConfigError)
Every stage in every ParallelGroup is a member of config.stages (enforced at construction time)
No ReviewStage appears in more than one ParallelGroup (enforced at construction time)
PACT key constants are module-level: PACT_CIRCUIT_INIT, PACT_CIRCUIT_RUN, PACT_CIRCUIT_RESOLVE_PLAN, PACT_CIRCUIT_INVOKE_REVIEWER, PACT_CIRCUIT_GET_TIMEOUT, PACT_CIRCUIT_GET_PLAN
All Pydantic models used by circuit are frozen=True (immutable after construction)
Chronicler events include the PACT key of the emitting method for Sentinel attribution
ClockProvider injection enables fully deterministic testing — no real time dependencies when clock is provided

data_access.rationale: Reads ReviewRequest objects containing classified DiffHunks (which may reference PII-adjacent file paths or code content) but does not inspect or persist the code content itself — it only passes DiffHunks through to reviewer implementations. Writes INTERNAL execution telemetry via Chronicler events (stage names, durations, error messages). No PII is written or stored by this component.

side_effects.rationale: Emits stage.started and stage.complete Chronicler events at every stage boundary for observability and governance audit trail. Fire-and-forget — failures do not block pipeline.
Delegates classified DiffHunks to registered ReviewerProtocol implementations for each configured stage. Each invocation is wrapped in circuit-breaker timeout protection.

authority.rationale: The circuit module is the authoritative owner of execution plan resolution, stage routing topology, circuit-breaker degradation policy, and the ordering guarantees for assessment results. No other component determines how reviewers are orchestrated or how failures are absorbed.
